sentence: O tempo não está nada bom, parece que vai chover.
ipa: o ˈtẽpu ˈnɐ̃w̃ eˈʃta ˈnadɐ ˈbõ pɐˈɾjɛsɨ kɨ ˈbaj ʃuˈbjɛɾ
gloss_en: The weather is not good at all, it looks like it will rain.
notes: Northern betacism 'vai'/'chover' realised [b]/[ʃuˈbjɛɾ] per cintra1971; open tonic diphthong [jɛ] in 'parece' and 'chover' per brissos2018.

sentence: Estes livros novos custam trinta e cinco euros.
ipa: ˈjeʃtɨʒ ˈlibɾuʒ ˈnwɔbuʃ ˈkuʃtɐ̃ ˈtɾĩtɐ i ˈsĩku ˈewɾuʃ
gloss_en: These new books cost thirty-five euros.
notes: Close tonic diphthong 'estes' [ˈjeʃtɨʒ] per cintra1971; betacism 'livros'/'novos' [b] per segura2013; coda [ʃ] chiado inherited per mateus_dandrade2000.

sentence: A reunião com o avô é às nove desta noite.
ipa: ɐ ʁewniˈɐ̃w̃ kõ o ɐˈbwo ˈjɛ aʒ ˈnwɔbɨ ˈdjɛʃtɐ ˈnojtɨ
gloss_en: The meeting with grandfather is tonight at nine.
notes: Close [o] diphthong with betacism 'avô' [ɐˈbwo] per cintra1971; strong initial [ʁ] in 'reunião' per segura2013.

sentence: O meu primo vive numa vivenda perto do rio.
ipa: o ˈmew ˈpɾimu ˈbibɨ ˈnumɐ biˈbẽdɐ ˈpjɛɾtu du ˈʁiu
gloss_en: My cousin lives in a house near the river.
notes: Betacism 'vive'/'vivenda' [ˈbibɨ]/[biˈbẽdɐ] per cintra1971; strong [ʁ] in 'rio' per segura2013.

sentence: Vira à esquerda e segue sempre em frente até à ponte.
ipa: ˈbiɾɐ a eˈʃkjɛɾdɐ i ˈsɨɡɨ ˈsẽpɾ ẽ ˈfɾẽtɨ ɐˈtjɛ a ˈpõtɨ
gloss_en: Turn left and keep straight ahead to the bridge.
notes: Betacism imperative 'vira' [ˈbiɾɐ] per cintra1971; open diphthong [jɛ] in 'esquerda' and 'até' per brissos2018.

sentence: A carne de vaca com feijão estava mesmo deliciosa.
ipa: ɐ ˈkaɾnɨ dɨ ˈbakɐ kõ feˈjʒɐ̃w̃ eˈʃtabɐ ˈmjɛʃmu dɨlisiˈwɔzɐ
gloss_en: The beef with beans was really delicious.
notes: Betacism 'vaca'/'estava' [ˈbakɐ]/[eˈʃtabɐ] per cintra1971; 'ei' retention in 'feijão' [ej] per cintra1971; inherited coda [ʃ] per mateus_dandrade2000.

sentence: Onde é que fica a estação dos autocarros?
ipa: ˈwõdɨ ˈjɛ kɨ ˈfikɐ ɐ eʃtɐˈsɐ̃w̃ duz awtuˈkaʁuʃ
gloss_en: Where is the bus station?
notes: Strong [ʁ] in 'autocarros' per segura2013; nasal diphthong [ɐ̃w̃] in 'estação' and 'dos autocarros' voiced sandhi [z] inherited per mateus_dandrade2000.

sentence: Quantas pessoas vêm ao jantar esta noite?
ipa: kwɐ̃ˈtɐʃ pɨˈs̺wɔɐʒ ˈbjẽ ɐu ʒɐ̃ˈtaɾ ˈjeʃtɐ ˈnojtɨ
gloss_en: How many people are coming to dinner tonight?
notes: Betacism 'vêm' [ˈbjẽ] and close diphthong 'esta' [ˈjeʃtɐ] per cintra1971; apical intervocalic [s̺] in 'pessoas' documented per cintra1971; 'pessoas vêm' coda-s sandhi inherited per mateus_dandrade2000.

sentence: Se eu pudesse, comprava aquela casa velha no monte.
ipa: sɨ ˈew puˈdjɛs̺ɨ kõˈpɾabɐ ɐˈkjɛlɐ ˈkazɐ ˈbjɛʎɐ nu ˈmõtɨ
gloss_en: If I could, I would buy that old house on the hill.
notes: Betacism 'comprava'/'velha' [b] with palatal [ʎ] in 'velha' per cintra1971; open diphthong [jɛ] in 'pudesse'/'aquela' per brissos2018; 'se eu' liaison inherited per mateus_dandrade2000.

sentence: Ontem comprámos vinho verde e pães quentes na feira.
ipa: ˈwõtɨ̃ kõˈpɾamuʒ ˈbiɲu ˈbjɛɾd i ˈpɐ̃j̃ʃ kẽˈtɨʒ nɐ ˈfejɾɐ
gloss_en: Yesterday we bought green wine and warm bread at the market.
notes: Betacism 'vinho'/'verde' [ˈbiɲu]/[ˈbjɛɾdɨ] with palatal [ɲ] per cintra1971; 'ei' retention 'feira' [ˈfejɾɐ] per cintra1971; nasal diphthong [ɐ̃j̃] in 'pães' inherited per mateus_dandrade2000.

sentence: No próximo mês vamos de comboio até ao Porto ver o mar.
ipa: nu ˈpɾwɔʃimu ˈmjeʒ ˈbamuʒ dɨ kõˈboju ɐˈtjɛ ɐu ˈpwɔɾtu ˈbjɛɾ o ˈmaɾ
gloss_en: Next month we go by train to Porto to see the sea.
notes: Close [e] diphthong 'mês' [ˈmjeʒ] and the emblematic 'Porto' [ˈpwɔɾtu] per brissos2018; betacism 'vamos'/'ver' [b] per cintra1971; 'até ao' liaison inherited per mateus_dandrade2000.

sentence: Telefona ao avô quando chegares a Vila do Conde.
ipa: tɨlɨˈfwɔnɐ ɐu ɐˈbwo ˈkwɐ̃du ʃɨˈɡaɾɨz ɐ ˈbilɐ du ˈkõdɨ
gloss_en: Call grandfather when you get to Vila do Conde.
notes: Betacism 'Vila' [ˈbilɐ] and close [o] diphthong 'avô' [ɐˈbwo] per cintra1971; 'chegares a' voiced coda-s sandhi [z] inherited per mateus_dandrade2000.

sentence: Bom dia, então como é que tens passado, ó rapaz?
ipa: ˈbõ ˈdiɐ ẽˈtɐ̃w̃ ˈkwɔmu ˈjɛ kɨ ˈtẽʃ pɐˈs̺adu ˈwɔ ʁɐˈpaʃ
gloss_en: Good morning, so how have you been, lad?
notes: Strong initial [ʁ] in 'rapaz' per segura2013; open diphthong [wɔ] in 'como' per brissos2018; nasal diphthong [ɐ̃w̃] 'então' and apical [s̺] 'passado' per mateus_dandrade2000.

sentence: Mais vale um pássaro na mão que dois a voar.
ipa: ˈmajʒ ˈbalɨ ˈũ ˈpas̺ɐɾu nɐ ˈmɐ̃w̃ kɨ ˈdojz ɐ buˈaɾ
gloss_en: A bird in the hand is worth two in the bush.
notes: Betacism 'vale'/'voar' [ˈbalɨ]/[buˈaɾ] per cintra1971; coda [ʃ] 'mais' and nasal diphthong [ɐ̃w̃] 'mão' inherited per mateus_dandrade2000.

sentence: O carro corre depressa pela serra acima.
ipa: o ˈkaʁu ˈkwɔʁɨ dɨˈpɾjɛs̺ɐ pɨlɐ ˈsjɛʁɐ ɐˈsimɐ
gloss_en: The car races quickly up the mountain.
notes: Strong [ʁ] for the ⟨rr⟩ grapheme in 'carro'/'corre'/'serra' per segura2013; open diphthong [jɛ] in 'depressa'/'pela'/'serra' per brissos2018; 'serra acima' liaison inherited per mateus_dandrade2000.

sentence: O galo cantou primeiro, depois veio o sol.
ipa: o ˈɡalu kɐ̃ˈtow pɾiˈmejɾu dɨˈpojʒ ˈbeju o ˈswɔɫ
gloss_en: The rooster crowed first, then the sun came out.
notes: 'ou' retention 'cantou' [kɐ̃ˈtow] and 'ei' retention 'primeiro' [pɾiˈmejɾu] per cintra1971; betacism 'veio' [ˈbeju] per cintra1971; dark coda [ɫ] in 'sol' inherited per mateus_dandrade2000.

sentence: Ele mora perto do mar e trabalha no barco.
ipa: ˈjelɨ ˈmwɔɾɐ ˈpjɛɾtu du ˈmaɾ i tɾɐˈbaʎɐ nu ˈbaɾku
gloss_en: He lives near the sea and works on the boat.
notes: Close [e] diphthong 'ele' [ˈjelɨ] per cintra1971; open diphthong [wɔ] 'mora' and [jɛ] 'perto' per brissos2018; palatal [ʎ] in 'trabalha' inherited per mateus_dandrade2000.

sentence: O peixeiro velho vende peixe fresco lá no cais.
ipa: o peˈjʃejɾu ˈbjɛʎu ˈbẽdɨ ˈpejʃɨ ˈfɾjɛʃku ˈla nu ˈkajʃ
gloss_en: The old fishmonger sells fresh fish on the quay.
notes: 'ei' retention 'peixeiro'/'peixe' [ej] per cintra1971; betacism 'velho'/'vende' [b] with palatal [ʎ] in 'velho' per cintra1971; inherited coda [ʃ] per mateus_dandrade2000.

sentence: O sol brilha por cima do vale enquanto o gado pasta.
ipa: o ˈswɔɫ ˈbɾiʎɐ puɾ ˈsimɐ du ˈbal ẽˈkwɐ̃tu o ˈɡadu ˈpaʃtɐ
gloss_en: The sun shines over the valley while the cattle graze.
notes: Dark coda [ɫ] 'sol' and palatal [ʎ] 'brilha' inherited per mateus_dandrade2000; betacism 'vale' [ˈbal] per cintra1971; 'vale enquanto' liaison inherited per mateus_dandrade2000.

sentence: A festa foi ontem, mas o baile só acabou de madrugada.
ipa: ɐ ˈfjɛʃtɐ ˈfoj ˈwõtɨ̃ ˈmaz o ˈbajlɨ ˈswɔ ɐkɐˈbow dɨ mɐdɾuˈɡadɐ
gloss_en: The party was yesterday, but the dance only ended at dawn.
notes: 'ou' retention 'acabou' [ɐkɐˈbow] per cintra1971; open diphthong [jɛ] 'festa' and [wɔ] 'só' per brissos2018; 'mas o' coda-s sandhi site inherited per mateus_dandrade2000.
